summary: Iteration-2 BottomNav click-interception bug is FULLY RESOLVED. Standard Playwright page.click() on [data-testid=trigger-spike-btn] (NO JS bypass) now successfully triggers the alarm overlay within ~128ms with both correct red banners (Temperature spike detected: 34.2 °C, Humidity drop detected: 22.0 %), POST /api/simulator/spike is sent, and overlay auto-disappears at +4721ms (≈ MQTT round-trip + 3.5s spikeActiveUntil window). All 4 BottomNav items (dashboard/analytics/logs/settings) still navigate correctly, confirming pointer-events:none on .bottom-nav wrapper + pointer-events:auto on .bottom-nav__item children works as designed. Quick Action buttons (Trigger Alarm, View Analytics) are now reachable. Trigger-spike-btn now sits at viewport y≈689 (was y≈828 in iter2) due to .app-shell padding-bottom: 140px, and document.elementFromPoint at the button center returns SPAN inside the button (pointer-events:auto) — no longer a nav <a>. Iteration-2 RCA is fully mitigated.

issues: The 'Open Logs' Quick Action button is missing a data-testid attribute. Found data-testids on Quick Actions are only ['trigger-spike-btn', 'view-analytics-btn']. Add data-testid='open-logs-btn' (or similar) for testability parity with the other two Quick Action buttons.

action_items: (LOW) Add a data-testid attribute to the 'Open Logs' Quick Action button on Dashboard so it can be targeted in tests like trigger-spike-btn / view-analytics-btn.

(INFO ONLY) Auto-dismiss measured at +4721ms includes the 3.5s spikeActiveUntil window plus network round-trip and 100ms polling granularity. Strictly speaking the request said 'around 3.5-4.5s'; we measured 4.72s. Acceptable but if a tighter window is desired, set spikeActiveUntil to start in MqttContext immediately (optimistically) on triggerSpike() rather than after the synthetic message is received, so it does not include the POST round-trip.
critical_code_review_comments: index.css fix is clean and minimal: pointer-events:none on .bottom-nav wrapper + pointer-events:auto on .bottom-nav__item children is the correct CSS-only way to let underlying Quick Action buttons receive clicks while preserving each tab's clickability. This is preferred over raising z-index, which would have caused other layout side effects.
.app-shell padding-bottom: 140px is well-commented and gives generous clearance (BottomNav ~84px + safe-area). Consider env(safe-area-inset-bottom) on iOS web for parity with the Flutter design.
BottomNav still has z-index: 30 same as .appbar — fine since they don't overlap, but document this so future devs don't accidentally raise overlay z-index below 50 (modal-backdrop) and conflict.
Open Logs quick action missing data-testid is the only testability gap on Dashboard. All other interactive elements have stable data-testids.

MqttContext.js spikeActiveUntil + interval re-render is solid. Consider exposing isSpikeActive boolean directly in the context value for easier consumer use (currently AlarmOverlay re-derives from spikeActiveUntil).

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all 5 requested checks pass; one minor missing data-testid noted)

context_for_next_testing_agent: BottomNav z-index/pointer-events bug from iteration 2 is fully fixed. Verified via standard page.click() (NOT JS bypass): alarm-overlay renders at +128ms, banners correct ('Temperature spike detected: 34.2 °C', 'Humidity drop detected: 22.0 %'), POST /api/simulator/spike fires, overlay auto-dismisses at ~4.7s. All 4 nav items (nav-dashboard/nav-analytics/nav-logs/nav-settings) navigate correctly. Quick Actions trigger-spike-btn and view-analytics-btn are reachable. Only minor finding: 'Open Logs' Quick Action button lacks a data-testid. Backend regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_iot_api.py was not re-run in this iteration (frontend-only review_request) but was passing in iter 2.
rca of the issue: PREVIOUSLY FIXED. Iter-2 root cause: BottomNav wrapper captured pointer events across its full fixed-position area (which extended above the visible icon row at viewport 1280×900 because Quick Actions sat at y≈828). Fix applied in /app/frontend/src/index.css: (1) .bottom-nav { pointer-events: none } so the wrapper passes clicks through to elements below, (2) .bottom-nav__item { pointer-events: auto } so the actual nav links still receive clicks, (3) .app-shell { padding-bottom: 140px } so Quick Actions render above the BottomNav rectangle (button center now at y≈689). Verified: page.click('[data-testid=trigger-spike-btn]') now hits the button (topmost element at center is SPAN inside the button, pointer-events=auto), POST /api/simulator/spike fires, alarm-overlay renders with correct banners, auto-dismisses ~4.7s later. All 4 nav tabs still navigate correctly.
